vocab.meaning: chọn, ưu tiên chọn cái gì đó
vocab.example: Most office workers tend to go for comfort since they are on their feet for the better part of the day.

vocab.meaning: khoe ra, phô trương (vẻ đẹp, phong cách)
vocab.example: Younger generations often wear avant-garde sneakers just to show off their unique sense of style.
When attending formal events, people usually opt for high-end aesthetic over practicality.

vocab.meaning: theo kịp (xu hướng, mốt thời trang)
vocab.example: Many fashionistas feel pressured to keep up with the latest trends, even if the shoes are incredibly painful.

vocab.meaning: trở nên nổi bật, khác biệt
vocab.example: Extravagant footwear is a great way to stand out in a crowd, despite being far from ergonomic.

vocab.meaning: mòn, hỏng (sau một thời gian sử dụng)
vocab.example: I find that cheap but good-looking shoes tend to wear out much faster than high-quality, comfortable ones.

vocab.meaning: chịu đựng (sự khó chịu, đau đớn)
vocab.example: Some individuals are willing to put up with blisters as long as their shoes complement their outfit perfectly.

vocab.meaning: cuối cùng thì, kết cục là (thường không định trước)
vocab.example: If you prioritize looks too much, you might end up with chronic foot pain in the long run.

vocab.meaning: phối đồ, kết hợp linh hoạt
vocab.example: Athleisure is popular now because it allows people to mix and match stylish trainers with professional attire.

vocab.meaning: cắt giảm, bớt chú trọng vào cái gì
vocab.example: As people get older, they often scale back on flashy designs and prioritize orthopedic support instead.
